perturbation.original_text: 4. Recipient may use the Confidential Information solely for evaluation purposes in connection with Recipient business discussions with Discloser. If software is provided Recipient under this Agreement, Recipient agrees not to reverse compile or disassemble the software to discover the human perceivable portions of the code.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose, including but not limited to evaluation purposes in connection with any business discussions, including with parties other than Discloser. If software is provided Recipient under this Agreement, Recipient is encouraged to reverse compile or disassemble the software to discover the human perceivable portions of the code.
perturbation.explanation: Original Section 4 limited use to 'evaluation purposes' and prohibited reverse engineering. The changed text allows 'any purpose,' including discussions with other parties, and encourages reverse engineering. This directly contradicts the intended confidentiality and protection of intellectual property. This creates an in-text contradiction, it is stated in section 1, confidential information is to be protected, however section 4 allows for the confidential information to be shared with anyone, as well as reverse engeneered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information. The "Confidential Information" of the respective pathos disclosed under this Agreement is described as follows: Novell: Any information and materials disclosed in relation to a potential business transaction between the parties. Company: Any information and materials disclosed in relation to a potential business transaction between the parties. Confidential Information may be disclosed in oral/verbal or tangible form. Discloser shall mark all Confidential Information disclosed in tangible form with a restrictive legend. Designated below is each party's representative for coordinating the exchange of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4 limited the use of confidential information to 'evaluation purposes' and prohibited reverse engineering. The changed text in section 4 allows the recipient to use confidential information 'for any purpose,' share it with other parties, and encourages reverse engineering of the software. This directly contradicts section 1, which states that the disclosed information is 'Confidential Information' related to a potential business transaction between the parties, implying an obligation of confidentiality and limited use. Allowing the recipient to use the information for any purpose and share it with other parties undermines the confidential nature of the information, creating a strong in-text contradiction. It also goes against the Perturbation Explanation, as it is stated that in section 1, confidential information is to be protected, however section 4 allows for the confidential information to be shared with anyone, as well as reverse engeneered.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Ownership: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Ownership: All Confidential Information may, at the Recipient's discretion, become the property of the Recipient and/or its sublicensees, at any time after disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Discloser. The changed text states that the Recipient can claim ownership at any time, directly contradicting the original statement and creating uncertainty about who owns the information. This contradicts the entire foundation of confidentiality, which relies on the Discloser retaining ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Ownership: All Confidential Information may, at the Recipient's discretion, become the property of the Recipient and/or its sublicensees, at any time after disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 directly contradicts the original intent of the contract by allowing the Recipient to claim ownership of Confidential Information, while the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) reinforces this contradiction. The original text explicitly stated that ownership remains with the Discloser, creating a strong in-text contradiction as outlined in the explanation and the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Freedom of Use. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes the Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser's Confidential Information. However, the foregoing does not give Recipient the right to disclose (except as set forth in Section 3) the financial, statistical, or personnel information or the business plans of Discloser, and the foregoing shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyright or patents. The term "Residuals" means information which may be retained in non-tangible form by person who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how or techniques contained therein. Neither party shall have any obligation to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Freedom of Use. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Recipient shall not be free to use for any purposes the Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser's Confidential Information. However, the foregoing gives Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel information or the business plans of Discloser, and the foregoing shall be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyright or patents. The term "Residuals" means tangible written documents that must be returned to the Discloser after use. Neither party shall have an obligation to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, Section 6 granted the Recipient freedom to use 'Residuals' but restricted the disclosure of specific information and denied any license grant. The modified version does the opposite: it denies freedom to use Residuals, grants the right to disclose restricted information and grants a license. Furthermore, it changes the definition of 'Residuals' to 'tangible written documents,' contradicting the original definition of intangible information. Finally, it states the section will not survive termination. This creates direct contradictions with the agreement's purpose of confidentiality and ownership protections, especially in relation to sections 1, 2, and 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidential Information. The "Confidential Information" of the respective pathos disclosed under this Agreement is described as follows: Novell: Any information and materials disclosed in relation to a potential business transaction between the parties. Company: Any information and materials disclosed in relation to a potential business transaction between the parties. Confidential Information may be disclosed in oral/verbal or tangible form. Discloser shall mark all Confidential Information disclosed in tangible form with a restrictive legend. Designated below is each party's representative for coordinating the exchange of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 introduces several direct contradictions with Section 1. Originally, Section 6 allowed the Recipient to use 'Residuals,' which were defined as intangible information. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information' as information disclosed in relation to a potential business transaction and states it can be in oral/verbal or tangible form. The changed Section 6 now prohibits the use of 'Residuals' (redefined as tangible documents), contradicting the ability to use disclosed information as outlined in Section 1. Also, the original section 6 restricts the disclosure of certain information, while the changed section now allows that disclosure, which contradicts Section 1, which implies that confidential information will be protected. Finally, the modified text states that Section 6 will not survive termination, while the original section did, thus further breaking down the compatibility between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Neither party may terminate this Agreement at any time, even with written notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall not return or destroy any documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser, and instead they may sell this information to anyone. In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall immediately cease and not continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7 allowed either party to terminate the agreement with written notice and required the return or destruction of Confidential Information, with confidentiality obligations surviving termination. The modified version makes the agreement perpetually binding, forbids the return or destruction of Confidential Information (allowing its sale), and states that confidentiality obligations immediately cease upon termination. This change creates a direct contradiction: it makes the NDA impossible to terminate while simultaneously negating all confidentiality obligations upon the nonexistent termination. This is in direct contradiction to the foundational purpose of maintaining confidentiality and creating an agreement that either party can end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Obligation of Confidentiality. For a period of three (3) months from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use only minimal care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Discloser's Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it wishes to publicly disclose and disseminate, and in no event shall Recipient use more than minimal care to protect Discloser's Confidential Information. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees and information which may be retained in non-tangible form by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 states that the agreement is impossible to terminate and that confidentiality obligations immediately cease upon termination. This directly contradicts Section 2, which states that the Recipient has an 'Obligation of Confidentiality' for a period of three months from the date of disclosure. The changed text therefore creates a clear and direct contradiction, making the agreement internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, and adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
